articles.title: Creusot helps you prove your Rust code is correct
articles.summary: Creusot helps you prove your Rust code is correct by translating it into Coma, an intermediate verification language of the Why3 Platform, which then leverages the full power of Why3 to discharge verification conditions. This allows users to take their code further and verify it does the correct thing. Creusot works by verifying panics, overflows, and assertion failures in Rust code, making it a valuable tool for developers who want to ensure their code is safe and reliable.

The summit attendees represented various projects and providers, including Delta Chat, FreePG, Hockeypuck, keys.openpgp.org, Proton, Ripasso, Sequoia-PGP, Signstar, Thunderbird, VOA, and others. Governance and the openpgp.org Domain are also topics of discussion, with a proposed constitutional change to the voting body and the Wau Holland Foundation as a trustee being considered.

This requires developers to verify if an instance is obsolete before calling generic functions that rely on inlined readers.
